content.problemStatement:
An array AAA of length NNN is said to be good if A1⋅A2⋅…AN=A1+A2+…+ANA_1 \cdot A_2 \cdot \ldots A_N = A_1 + A_2 + \ldots + A_NA1​⋅A2​⋅…AN​=A1​+A2​+…+AN​, i.e. product equals sum.

Given an array AAA of length NNN, count how many subarrays of AAA are good. Formally, find the number of pairs (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) such that:


1≤L≤R≤N1 \le L \le R \le N1≤L≤R≤N
The array [AL,AL+1,AL+2,…,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, A_{L + 2}, \ldots, A_R][AL​,AL+1​,AL+2​,…,AR​] is good.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subarrays of AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1 : The good subarrays are [2],[1],[3][2], [1], [3][2],[1],[3] and [2,1,3][2, 1, 3][2,1,3].
Test Case 2 : [2][2][2] and [2,2][2, 2][2,2] are good. [2][2][2] occurs 444 times, and [2,2][2, 2][2,2] occurs 333 times.
